product_overview: Parlour is a premium, visually rich beauty salon / studio website built with React, GSAP, Framer Motion, Lenis, and Supabase. The site features a branded intro animation, hero section, horizontal studio work gallery, about section, services catalog with multi-select, appointment booking form with custom calendar, archive/profile section, reserve section, and an animated footer. All content is dynamically fetched from Supabase with real-time subscription support, and every interaction has micro-animations and premium motion design.
core_goals: Deliver an animated premium intro that reveals the main site content.
Show hero section with dynamic heading, subtitle, and status labels from Supabase.
Display a horizontally-scrollable studio work gallery with drag support and a full-screen 'All Works' modal.
Showcase the About section with philosophy text, days, hours, and a tilted card image.
Provide a real-time services catalog with category filters and multi-select functionality.
Enable a complete multi-step appointment booking: select services → pick date → fill contact form → submit → get confirmation ticket.
Show archive/profile section with establishment stats, ratings, and brand mission.
Display reserve/CTA section and a footer with contact links and social icons.
All buttons, links, and interactive elements must function correctly on mobile (320px+), tablet (768px+), laptop (1024px+), and desktop (1280px+).
No broken layouts, JavaScript errors, or missing content on any viewport.

features.name: Premium Intro Animation
features.description: On page load, a full-screen branded intro animation (PremiumIntro) plays with a scanning bar effect. After completion, the animation unmounts and the main site content fades in.
user_flows: Navigate to / → See full-screen intro animation play → Animation completes automatically → Main site content fades in → Page becomes scrollable and interactive
Navigate to / on mobile (375px) → Intro fills the screen → Scanning animation is visible and not clipped → Main content appears after completion
Navigate to / → Intro animation starts → Animation encounters an error → Fallback static content or the intro skips to main site without breaking the page
acceptance_criteria: Intro animation displays on initial page load
Intro auto-completes and transitions to main content
Main site navigation and content are NOT visible during the intro
Intro is visible and not clipped on mobile, tablet, and desktop viewports
No JavaScript errors during or after intro

features.name: Navigation Bar
features.description: Fixed glassmorphism navigation bar at the top with logo on the left and a hamburger menu button on the right. Clicking the hamburger opens a full-height drawer with animated nav items. Clicking a nav item smooth-scrolls to that section and closes the drawer.
user_flows: Load page → See nav bar fixed at top with logo and MENU button → Click MENU → See drawer slide in from the right with NAVIGATION label and nav links → Click 'HOME' → Drawer closes and page smooth-scrolls to hero section
Open nav drawer → Click 'STUDIO' → Drawer closes and page scrolls to Studio Work section
Open nav drawer → Click 'ABOUT' → Drawer closes and page scrolls to About section
Open nav drawer → Click 'CATALOG' → Drawer closes and page scrolls to Services Catalog section
Open nav drawer → Click 'RESERVE NOW' → Drawer closes and page scrolls to Reserve section
Open nav drawer → Click the X (close) button in the drawer → Drawer closes without navigating
Open nav drawer → Click the backdrop (outside drawer) → Drawer closes
Click the logo in the nav bar → Page scrolls back to hero / top of page
On mobile (375px): nav bar visible, logo and menu button are properly sized → Open drawer → Drawer is 320px wide, not overflowing
On tablet (768px): nav bar visible → drawer opens properly
On desktop (1280px): nav bar visible → drawer opens with 450px width
acceptance_criteria: Nav bar visible and fixed on all screen sizes
Hamburger button shows 'MENU' text on sm+ screens
Drawer slides in from right with staggered nav item animations
All 5 nav links scroll to correct sections
X button and backdrop click close the drawer
Logo click scrolls to top / hero section
No layout overflow on 375px mobile
Nav bar has correct glassmorphism styling

features.name: Floating Book Now & WhatsApp Buttons
features.description: A fixed floating button group in the bottom-right corner of the screen. Contains a green WhatsApp button (opens WhatsApp link) and a pink 'BOOK NOW' button. When services are selected, the BOOK NOW button updates to show 'BOOK N SESSION(S)'. Clicking the book button triggers a page transition animation and reveals the booking form.
user_flows: Load main site → See WhatsApp (green) and BOOK NOW (pink) floating buttons at bottom-right → Hover over BOOK NOW → Button scales up slightly
Click WhatsApp button → New tab/window opens with WhatsApp chat URL
Click BOOK NOW with no services selected → Booking form section appears below the reserve section with SVG wave transition animation
Select 2 services in the catalog → Floating button updates to 'BOOK 2 SESSIONS' → Click it → Scrolls to booking form
Click BOOK NOW when booking form is already visible → Page smooth-scrolls to the booking form (does not re-open)
On mobile (375px): Both floating buttons are visible and sized appropriately (p-3 padding), not overlapping other content
On desktop: Buttons use p-4 padding and are larger (md:p-4)
acceptance_criteria: Both buttons visible and clickable on all viewports
WhatsApp button opens correct URL in new tab
BOOK NOW triggers wave transition and reveals booking section
Button text updates dynamically based on selected services count
Buttons scale on hover/tap
Buttons do not overlap or block critical page content

features.name: Hero Section
features.description: Full-screen hero section with animated background (HeroSbg), dashed border overlay, and text content on the left side. Displays dynamic main heading, subtitle, and status label fetched from Supabase (section: 'header'). Features a diagonal decorative line and fade-in motion animation.
user_flows: Load site → After intro → See hero section filling the full viewport height → Animated background playing → Main heading visible → Subtitle and status label visible
Supabase has header.main_heading = 'PARLOUR BEAUTY' → Hero heading shows 'PARLOUR BEAUTY' correctly
Supabase unavailable → Hero falls back to local content 'YOUR BUSINESS NAME' from constants.tsx
On mobile (375px): Hero text is clamp-sized and readable, not overflowing the viewport
On tablet (768px): Hero text is larger, decorative lines appear
On desktop (1280px): Hero shows full layout with large heading and side decorative line
acceptance_criteria: Hero fills 100vh on all devices
Main heading, subtitle, and status label are rendered
Dynamic content from Supabase overrides local fallback
Animated background (HeroSbg) renders without errors

features.name: Studio Work Horizontal Gallery
features.description: A horizontal scrolling gallery section showing portfolio images from Supabase. On desktop: images are draggable with spring momentum and bounce at edges. On mobile/tablet: native scroll with snap. A 'See All Works' button opens a full-screen modal showing all gallery items. Section has a vertical 'STUDIO WORK' text on the left sidebar.
user_flows: Scroll to Studio Work section → See horizontally scrollable gallery cards → On desktop: click-drag horizontally → Images scroll with momentum → Release at edge → Spring bounce back
On mobile: scroll gallery cards horizontally with touch gesture → Images snap to nearest card
Click image card → No direct action (image is not individually clickable to open modal)
Click 'See All Works' button → Full-screen modal opens with all gallery images in a grid layout
In 'See All Works' modal → Scroll down to see all images → Click the X button to close modal
In modal → Click outside any image (on the dark overlay at top) → Modal closes
In modal → Images are shown in grayscale → Hover over image → Color photo with title and category overlay appears
Gallery images load from Supabase → Images display correctly with title text at bottom

On mobile (375px): Gallery cards are 280x350px, horizontally scrollable, 'See All Works' button is below the gallery
On desktop (1280px): Gallery cards are larger (500x650px), drag interaction works with mouse
acceptance_criteria: Gallery section renders without errors

features.name: About Section
features.description: A section with left-aligned text including the brand philosophy headline 'CRAFTED IN CHAOS', philosophy description, days of operation, and hours of operation. On the right side is a TiltedCard component with a tilt-on-hover effect showing an about image from Supabase. Content is fetched from Supabase (section: 'about').
user_flows: Scroll to About section → See 'CRAFTED IN CHAOS' heading → See philosophy text → See 'AVAILABLE // DAYS' and 'TIMING // SLOTS' labels with values → See tilted card on the right with an image
Supabase about.philosophy = 'We craft identity, not just style.' → About section displays this text
Supabase about.days = 'SUN — FRI' and about.hours = '9:00 AM — 9:00 PM' → Section displays updated days and hours
Hover over TiltedCard → Card tilts on mouse movement → Looks 3D/interactive
On mobile (375px): Tilted card is positioned within visible area, text reads correctly, philosophy text is max-width constrained
On desktop (1280px): About section shows full two-column layout with large heading and image card
acceptance_criteria: About section renders with heading, philosophy, days, and hours

features.name: Services Catalog with Category Filters
features.description: A full-width catalog section showing all services fetched from Supabase, grouped by category. Category filter buttons at the top ('ALL' plus each unique category) allow filtering. Clicking a service card toggles its selection state (highlighted with pink border and checkmark). Multiple services can be selected simultaneously. When services are selected, a total price bar appears with a 'BOOK N SESSION(S)' button.
user_flows: Scroll to Catalog section → See 'THE CATALOG' heading → See category filter buttons (ALL, HAIR, MAKEUP, etc.) → See service cards grouped by category
Click 'HAIR' category filter → Only hair services are shown, button gets white underline/active state
Click 'ALL' filter → All services from all categories are displayed again
Click a service card → Card becomes highlighted with pink border and checkmark in top-right corner → Text turns pink
Click same service card again → Card deselects, returns to default state
Select 2 services → See 'NICE CHOICE!' encouragement message appear above → See total investment amount displayed → See 'BOOK 2 SESSIONS' pink button
Select 3 services → Message updates to 'You're building an epic combo! 3 services selected' → Total price updated
Click 'BOOK 2 SESSIONS' from catalog → Wave transition animation plays → Booking form section appears
No services loaded from Supabase → Empty state with SVG icon and 'Services Loading...' text
On mobile (375px): Service cards in 1-column grid, tap to select works
On tablet (768px): 2-column grid of service cards
On desktop (1280px): 3-column grid of service cards
acceptance_criteria: Catalog section renders with heading and catalog label
All services from Supabase are displayed with title, duration, and price
Category filters dynamically generated from unique service categories

features.name: Appointment Booking Form
features.description: A multi-step booking form revealed after clicking BOOK NOW. Contains: Name field, Email field (with email format validation), Phone/Coms field (numbers/+/- only), Date field with a calendar popup and manual text input option, Services panel for adding services, and a Google Maps embed (desktop only at form level, mobile after button). On submit, data is sent to Supabase 'appointments' table. On success, a confirmation ticket is shown.
user_flows: Trigger booking form → See 'BOOK NOW' heading with 'BEGIN YOUR TRANSFORMATION' label → See 3-column form grid with Name, Email, Phone, Date, Services, Map fields
Click the calendar icon next to the date field → SimpleCalendar popup opens → Select a date → Calendar closes → Date displays in DD-MM-YYYY format in the date field
Manually type a date in the date field → Date field updates → Calendar is not triggered
Click 'ADD SERVICES' button in the services section → ServicePanel popup opens → Toggle services on/off → Selected services appear as items in the booking form
Remove a selected service by clicking the ✕ icon next to it in the booking form → Service removed, total price updated
Fill in Name, Email, Phone, select Date and Services → Click 'START SESSION' button → Button scales on hover → Form submits → Booking saved to Supabase → Confirmation ticket appears
Click 'START SESSION' with Name empty → 'FILL THIS FIRST' error message appears below Name field with vibration animation
Click 'START SESSION' with Email as 'invalidemail' → 'INVALID EMAIL FORMAT' error message appears below Email field
Click 'START SESSION' with Email empty → 'FILL THIS FIRST' error appears below Email field
Click 'START SESSION' with Phone empty → 'FILL THIS FIRST' error appears below Coms field
Click 'START SESSION' with Date empty → 'SELECT DATE' error appears
Click 'START SESSION' with no services selected → 'SELECT PROTOCOL' error appears
All fields missing → All error messages appear simultaneously with vibration animation
Start filling a field after error → Error for that field clears immediately as user types
Supabase save fails → Error message '⚠️ Failed to save appointment: [error text]' appears below the submit button
On mobile (375px): Form is 1-column layout, date field and services field are full width, Map field is hidden (appears after submit button on mobile), all form fields are tappable
On desktop (1280px): Form is 3-column grid, Google Map iframe is visible in the grid alongside other form fields
acceptance_criteria: Booking form appears after clicking BOOK NOW with wave animation
All 5 form fields are rendered: Name, Email, Phone, Date, Services
Calendar popup opens on calendar icon click and closes on date selection
ServicePanel popup opens on ADD SERVICES click
Selected services displayed in form with ✕ remove option

Form validates all required fields before submission
Correct error messages appear under each invalid field

Vibration animation triggers on validation failure
Successful submission saves to Supabase and shows confirmation

Google Map shows on mobile AFTER the START SESSION button

features.name: Appointment Confirmation Ticket
features.description: After successful booking submission, a confirmation ticket is displayed showing: booking name, email, phone, date, total investment, selected services list, and a random booking ID. Two action buttons: 'CANCEL' resets the form, 'DOWNLOAD' saves the ticket as a PNG image using html2canvas.
user_flows: Submit valid booking → Confirmation section appears → See 'PROTOCOL INITIATED' heading → See ticket with 'ESTILO BEAUTY SALON' label and 'CONFIRMED' badge → See all booking details filled in correctly → See CANCEL and DOWNLOAD buttons
Click DOWNLOAD button → Ticket is downloaded as appointment.png image file → Form resets
Click CANCEL button → Confirmation view closes → Form resets to empty state (name, email, phone, date, services all cleared)
On mobile (375px): Ticket is max-width 95% of screen, scrollable if content overflows, CANCEL and DOWNLOAD buttons are side by side
On desktop: Ticket is centered with max-width 600px
acceptance_criteria: Confirmation appears after successful Supabase save
All booking details are shown correctly on the ticket
'CONFIRMED' badge is visible on the ticket
CANCEL resets form and hides confirmation
DOWNLOAD creates and downloads a PNG file named 'appointment.png'
Ticket is responsive and readable on mobile

features.name: Archive / Studio Profile Section
features.description: A section below the booking form area showing the studio's profile with: personnel record label, team name, professional title, and a quote on the left. On the right: mission label, mission heading, and 4 animated stat counters (Established year, Staff Count, Success Rate, Ratings). All content from Supabase (section: 'profile'). Stats animate up from 0 using CountUpText component when scrolled into view.

On desktop (1280px): 12-column grid, left profile and right stats side by side

All contact info from Supabase (section: 'footer').

On mobile (375px): Footer top row shows social icons, copyright text is readable, footer title text scales with clamp
On desktop: Footer top row has correct flex layout

features.name: Supabase Real-Time Content Sync
features.description: The entire site subscribes to real-time Postgres changes from Supabase for site_content, services, and gallery tables. When the admin updates content in Supabase, the website automatically re-fetches and shows updated content without a page reload.
user_flows: Load the website → Content is fetched from Supabase on mount → Site displays Supabase content
Tab is in background → User switches back to tab → Window focus event fires → Content re-fetched from Supabase
Supabase is unreachable → Site falls back to local constants.tsx content → No crash or blank page
acceptance_criteria: Site loads with Supabase content
Real-time channel subscriptions are set up for site_content, services, gallery
Window focus re-fetches content
Local fallback content shows if Supabase fails

features.name: Responsive Design - Mobile (320px - 767px)
features.description: The entire website must be fully functional and visually correct on mobile devices from 375px width. All sections must stack vertically, text must be legible (clamp-sized), touch interactions must work, no horizontal overflow or scroll.
user_flows: Open site on 375px wide viewport → Intro animation fills screen → Main site loads → Hero section fills viewport with readable text → Scroll down → Studio gallery horizontally scrollable with touch → About section stacked with philosophy text and card → Catalog services in 1-column grid → Services are tap-to-select → Reserve section visible → Booking form accessible → Footer visible with social icons
Open site on 320px wide viewport → No horizontal scrollbar → All text readable → All buttons tappable
acceptance_criteria: No horizontal overflow on 375px viewport
All text is readable (minimum 12px)
All buttons have minimum touch-target size (44x44px recommended)
Gallery horizontal scroll works with touch
Booking form fields are tappable and keyboard-activatable
Nav drawer is 320px wide on mobile, not overflowing
Gallery modal is full-screen and scrollable on mobile

features.name: Responsive Design - Tablet (768px - 1023px)
features.description: The website adapts to tablet viewports. Two-column layouts activate for service catalog and profile section. Gallery cards scale to mid-size. Navigation remains the drawer style.
user_flows: Open site on 768px wide viewport → Hero text is larger than mobile → Catalog services in 2-column grid → Booking form shows 2-column layout → Footer shows correct layout → Gallery card sizes at sm: breakpoint
Open site on 834px (iPad) → All layouts look correct with no overflow
acceptance_criteria: 2-column service catalog grid on tablet
2-column booking form on tablet
Gallery modal 2-column grid on tablet
Gallery cards scale to sm: sizes (350x450px)

features.name: Responsive Design - Laptop / Desktop (1024px+)
features.description: Full desktop layout with 3-column service grid, 3-column booking form grid, large gallery cards, 12-column profile section, and large hero text. Drag interactions for gallery work with mouse.
user_flows: Open site on 1280px wide viewport → Hero has large heading (clamp up to 9rem) → Gallery cards are 500x650px → Studio gallery drag with mouse works → Catalog in 3-column grid → Booking form in 3-column grid with map visible → Profile in 12-column layout
Open site on 1920px wide viewport → All clamp-sized fonts remain bounded → No stretched or broken layouts
acceptance_criteria: 3-column service grid on desktop (lg:grid-cols-3)
3-column booking form on desktop
Google Map visible in booking form grid on desktop
Mouse drag works for studio gallery with momentum
Profile section uses 12-column grid with correct proportions
Gallery cards use correct lg: and xl: breakpoint sizes

features.name: Error & Edge Case Handling
features.description: All error states, empty states, and edge cases must be handled gracefully without crashing the page.
user_flows: Supabase API down → All fetch calls fail → Site uses local constants.tsx fallback data → No crash, no blank sections
Gallery table is empty → Studio Work section shows an empty slider with no cards → 'See All Works' modal shows empty grid → No error
Services table is empty → Catalog shows spinning/loading SVG with 'Services Loading...' text → No catalog items shown → No error
Book appointment form submitted with all fields valid but Supabase save fails due to network error → Error message shown: '⚠️ Failed to save appointment: [error]' → Form is NOT reset → User can retry
Booking form submitted without services selected → 'SELECT PROTOCOL' error appears → Form does not submit
Invalid email submitted → 'INVALID EMAIL FORMAT' error appears, form does not submit
Gallery modal opened when there are 0 gallery items → Empty grid shown with '0 WORKS DISPLAYED' footer
Phone field does not allow letters — only digits, +, spaces, and dashes are accepted
Navigator to a section that doesn't exist via nav → Graceful fallback (scroll does not crash)
acceptance_criteria: Site does not crash when Supabase is unavailable
Empty gallery shows no error
Empty services shows loading state, no crash
All form validation errors are shown correctly
Supabase submission failure shows error message without resetting form
Phone field only accepts numeric characters + and dash
Error messages vibrate/animate on all fields simultaneously when submit is clicked with multiple empty fields

routes.description: Single-page application — all sections on one route: intro, hero, studio-work, about, catalog, footer (reserve CTA), appointment form, confirmation, archive-details, and footer

sections.description: PremiumIntro animation component — plays on load, then unmounts
Full-screen hero with HeroSbg background, main heading, subtitle, status label
Horizontal gallery with drag scroll, GSAP parallax on cards, 'See All Works' button
Brand philosophy section with tilted card image, days, hours from Supabase
Services catalog with category filters, multi-select, total price, book button
Pink Reserve Now CTA section with book appointment button
Multi-step booking form with calendar, service panel, map embed, validation
Booking confirmation ticket with download and cancel buttons
Studio profile section with animated stat counters
White footer with contact links, social icons, animated FooterTitle

key_components.role: Fixed nav bar with GSAP drawer animation
Custom calendar popup for date selection (lazy loaded)
3D tilt card for about section image
Custom date text input with validation
All Supabase fetch calls (footer, bookNow, services, gallery, navigation)

api_calls.description: Fetch footer text and contact info
Fetch booking form settings and map embed URL
Fetch all site content (header, about, catalog, profile, studio, etc.)
Fetch all salon services with title, category, price, duration
Submit appointment booking with name, email, phone, services, date, price, status

known_limitations: Google Maps iframe requires a valid map_embed URL set in Supabase site_content (book_now section) to display correctly
Gallery drag uses native scrollLeft on mobile for touch support; GSAP Draggable is used on desktop only
Ticket download uses html2canvas which requires DOM access and may have cross-origin issues with external fonts
USE_LOCAL_CONTENT_ONLY in constants.tsx when set to true skips all Supabase fetches and uses local fallback
ServicePanel and SimpleCalendar are lazy-loaded; slow networks may show 'LOADING...' fallback briefly
